heading: 9.7.14.5.11.Matrix Fragments formma.m16n8k64
content:
A warp executing `mma.m16n8k64` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k64`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.s4` / `.u4` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a30, a31 |
  | `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.e2m1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a30, a31 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 93](#mma-16864-a).

  



[Figure: mma-16864-A]
MMA .m16n8k64 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .u4/.s4/.e2m1 type

=============================================================

1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
   - Instruction: MMA .m16n8k64
   - Matrix: A (input matrix)
   - Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), or .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)
   - Matrix dimensions: M=16 rows, N=8 columns, K=64 columns (for matrix A, shape is M x K = 16 x 64)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows (R): 0 through 15 (matrix rows)
   - Columns (C): 0 through 63 (matrix K-dimension columns), split into two halves:
     Left half: columns 0..31 (subdivided: 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31)
     Right half: columns 32..63 (subdivided: 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63)
   - Lane identifier: %laneid:{fragments}

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ELEMENT MAPPING

   The layout is organized in two major row bands (rows 0-7 and rows 8-15) and two major column bands (columns 0-31 and columns 32-63).

   --- ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 0-31 ---

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Columns 0-7:   T0:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 8-15:  T1:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 16-23: T2:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 24-31: T3:  {a0, a1, .., a7}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Columns 0-7:   T4:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 8-15:  T5:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 16-23: T6:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 24-31: T7:  {a0, a1, .., a7}

   Rows 2-6: continuation pattern indicated by arrows (rows 2 through 6 follow the same pattern with threads T8-T27 implied by the arrow continuation)

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Columns 0-7:   T28: {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 8-15:  T29: {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 16-23: T30: {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 24-31: T31: {a0, a1, .., a7}

   --- ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 32-63 ---

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Columns 32-39: T0:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 40-47: T1:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 48-55: T2:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 56-63: T3:  {a16, .., a23}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Columns 32-39: T4:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 40-47: T5:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 48-55: T6:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 56-63: T7:  {a16, .., a23}

   Rows 2-6: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern continues)

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Columns 32-39: T28: {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 40-47: T29: {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 48-55: T30: {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 56-63: T31: {a16, .., a23}

   --- ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 0-31 ---

   Row 8 (matrix row 8):
     Columns 0-7:   T0:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 8-15:  T1:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 16-23: T2:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 24-31: T3:  {a8, .., a15}

   Row 9 (matrix row 9):
     Columns 0-7:   T4:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 8-15:  T5:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 16-23: T6:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 24-31: T7:  {a8, .., a15}

   Rows 10-14: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern continues)

   Row 15 (matrix row 15):
     Columns 0-7:   T28: {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 8-15:  T29: {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 16-23: T30: {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 24-31: T31: {a8, .., a15}

   --- ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 32-63 ---

   Row 8 (matrix row 8):
     Columns 32-39: T0:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 40-47: T1:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 48-55: T2:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 56-63: T3:  {a24, .., a31}

   Row 9 (matrix row 9):
     Columns 32-39: T4:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 40-47: T5:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 48-55: T6:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 56-63: T7:  {a24, .., a31}

   Rows 10-14: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern continues)

   Row 15 (matrix row 15):
     Columns 32-39: T28: {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 40-47: T29: {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 48-55: T30: {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 56-63: T31: {a24, .., a31}

4. ELEMENT GROUPING SUMMARY

   Each thread holds 4 groups of 8 elements:
     Group 1: a0..a7   (columns 0-31,  rows 0-7,  i.e., upper-left quadrant)
     Group 2: a8..a15  (columns 0-31,  rows 8-15, i.e., lower-left quadrant)
     Group 3: a16..a23 (columns 32-63, rows 0-7,  i.e., upper-right quadrant)
     Group 4: a24..a31 (columns 32-63, rows 8-15, i.e., lower-right quadrant)

   Total elements per thread: 32 elements (a0 through a31)

5. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN

   Threads T0-T3:   cover matrix rows 0 (one row per thread across 4 column groups of 8)
   Threads T4-T7:   cover matrix row 1
   Threads T8-T11:  cover matrix row 2
   Threads T12-T15: cover matrix row 3
   Threads T16-T19: cover matrix row 4
   Threads T20-T23: cover matrix row 5
   Threads T24-T27: cover matrix row 6
   Threads T28-T31: cover matrix row 7

   The same thread assignment repeats for rows 8-15 (threads T0-T31 cover both the upper 8 rows and lower 8 rows via different register groups a0-a7/a8-a15 and a16-a23/a24-a31).

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS

   For .u4/.s4 types: 8 four-bit elements are packed into one 32-bit register (8 x 4 bits = 32 bits).
   For .e2m1 type: 8 three-bit elements packed similarly into 32-bit registers.
   Each named group {a0,a1,..,a7}, {a8,..,a15}, {a16,..,a23}, {a24,..,a31} corresponds to one 32-bit register holding 8 sub-byte elements.
   Each thread therefore uses 4 registers total to hold its 32 matrix A elements.

MMA .m16n8k64 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .u4/.s4/.e2m1 type

=============================================================

1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
   - Instruction: MMA .m16n8k64
   - Matrix: A (input matrix)
   - Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), or .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)
   - Matrix dimensions: M=16 rows, N=8 columns, K=64 columns (for matrix A, shape is M x K = 16 x 64)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows (R): 0 through 15 (matrix rows)
   - Columns (C): 0 through 63 (matrix K-dimension columns), split into two halves:
     Left half: columns 0..31 (subdivided: 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31)
     Right half: columns 32..63 (subdivided: 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63)
   - Lane identifier: %laneid:{fragments}

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ELEMENT MAPPING

   The layout is organized in two major row bands (rows 0-7 and rows 8-15) and two major column bands (columns 0-31 and columns 32-63).

   --- ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 0-31 ---

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Columns 0-7:   T0:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 8-15:  T1:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 16-23: T2:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 24-31: T3:  {a0, a1, .., a7}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Columns 0-7:   T4:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 8-15:  T5:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 16-23: T6:  {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 24-31: T7:  {a0, a1, .., a7}

   Rows 2-6: continuation pattern indicated by arrows (rows 2 through 6 follow the same pattern with threads T8-T27 implied by the arrow continuation)

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Columns 0-7:   T28: {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 8-15:  T29: {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 16-23: T30: {a0, a1, .., a7}
     Columns 24-31: T31: {a0, a1, .., a7}

   --- ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 32-63 ---

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Columns 32-39: T0:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 40-47: T1:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 48-55: T2:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 56-63: T3:  {a16, .., a23}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Columns 32-39: T4:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 40-47: T5:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 48-55: T6:  {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 56-63: T7:  {a16, .., a23}

   Rows 2-6: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern continues)

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Columns 32-39: T28: {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 40-47: T29: {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 48-55: T30: {a16, .., a23}
     Columns 56-63: T31: {a16, .., a23}

   --- ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 0-31 ---

   Row 8 (matrix row 8):
     Columns 0-7:   T0:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 8-15:  T1:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 16-23: T2:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 24-31: T3:  {a8, .., a15}

   Row 9 (matrix row 9):
     Columns 0-7:   T4:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 8-15:  T5:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 16-23: T6:  {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 24-31: T7:  {a8, .., a15}

   Rows 10-14: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern continues)

   Row 15 (matrix row 15):
     Columns 0-7:   T28: {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 8-15:  T29: {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 16-23: T30: {a8, .., a15}
     Columns 24-31: T31: {a8, .., a15}

   --- ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 32-63 ---

   Row 8 (matrix row 8):
     Columns 32-39: T0:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 40-47: T1:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 48-55: T2:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 56-63: T3:  {a24, .., a31}

   Row 9 (matrix row 9):
     Columns 32-39: T4:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 40-47: T5:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 48-55: T6:  {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 56-63: T7:  {a24, .., a31}

   Rows 10-14: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern continues)

   Row 15 (matrix row 15):
     Columns 32-39: T28: {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 40-47: T29: {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 48-55: T30: {a24, .., a31}
     Columns 56-63: T31: {a24, .., a31}

4. ELEMENT GROUPING SUMMARY

   Each thread holds 4 groups of 8 elements:
     Group 1: a0..a7   (columns 0-31,  rows 0-7,  i.e., upper-left quadrant)
     Group 2: a8..a15  (columns 0-31,  rows 8-15, i.e., lower-left quadrant)
     Group 3: a16..a23 (columns 32-63, rows 0-7,  i.e., upper-right quadrant)
     Group 4: a24..a31 (columns 32-63, rows 8-15, i.e., lower-right quadrant)

   Total elements per thread: 32 elements (a0 through a31)

5. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN

   Threads T0-T3:   cover matrix rows 0 (one row per thread across 4 column groups of 8)
   Threads T4-T7:   cover matrix row 1
   Threads T8-T11:  cover matrix row 2
   Threads T12-T15: cover matrix row 3
   Threads T16-T19: cover matrix row 4
   Threads T20-T23: cover matrix row 5
   Threads T24-T27: cover matrix row 6
   Threads T28-T31: cover matrix row 7

   The same thread assignment repeats for rows 8-15 (threads T0-T31 cover both the upper 8 rows and lower 8 rows via different register groups a0-a7/a8-a15 and a16-a23/a24-a31).

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS

   For .u4/.s4 types: 8 four-bit elements are packed into one 32-bit register (8 x 4 bits = 32 bits).
   For .e2m1 type: 8 three-bit elements packed similarly into 32-bit registers.
   Each named group {a0,a1,..,a7}, {a8,..,a15}, {a16,..,a23}, {a24,..,a31} corresponds to one 32-bit register holding 8 sub-byte elements.
   Each thread therefore uses 4 registers total to hold its 32 matrix A elements.


  Figure 93 MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for matrix A with `.u4` / `.s4` / `.e2m1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =     groupID                                     for ai where 0 <= i < 8 || 16 <= i < 24
          groupID + 8                                   otherwise

  col =      (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7)        for ai where i < 16
             (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7) + 32   for ai where i >= 16
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.s4` / `.u4` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, …, b14, b15 |
  | `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.e2m1` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, …, b14, b15 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 94](#mma-16864-b-1)
  and [Figure 95](#mma-16864-b-2).

  



[Figure: mma-16864-B_1]
MMA .m16n8k64 Fragment Layout for Matrix B (rows 0-31), data types: .u4 / .s4 / .e2m1

OVERVIEW:
- Matrix: B
- Instruction: MMA .m16n8k64
- Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)
- This figure covers rows 0 through 31 of matrix B
- Grid structure: Rows represent groups of matrix rows, Columns (0, 1, 2, .., 7) represent register indices

GRID DIMENSIONS:
- Row groups: 4 groups (rows 0-7, rows 8-15, rows 16-23, rows 24-31)
- Columns shown: 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, indices 0 through 7)

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING:

Row group 0-7 (matrix rows 0 to 7):
  Column 0: Thread T0 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7 (8 elements packed in register)
  Column 1: Thread T4 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied continuation of pattern, arrow indicates column 2 maps to same pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T28 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row group 8-15 (matrix rows 8 to 15):
  Column 0: Thread T1 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T5 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T29 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row group 16-23 (matrix rows 16 to 23):
  Column 0: Thread T2 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T6 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T30 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row group 24-31 (matrix rows 24 to 31):
  Column 0: Thread T3 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T7 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, arrow from column 2 row 0-7 points down to column 2 row 24-31 indicating continuation)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T31 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN (column index to thread number):
  Column 0: T0 (rows 0-7), T1 (rows 8-15), T2 (rows 16-23), T3 (rows 24-31)
  Column 1: T4 (rows 0-7), T5 (rows 8-15), T6 (rows 16-23), T7 (rows 24-31)
  Column 2: (T8, T9, T10, T11 implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T28 (rows 0-7), T29 (rows 8-15), T30 (rows 16-23), T31 (rows 24-31)

General pattern: Thread number = (column_index * 4) + row_group_index
  where row_group_index = 0 for rows 0-7, 1 for rows 8-15, 2 for rows 16-23, 3 for rows 24-31

REGISTER PACKING:
Each thread holds 8 elements per register entry: b0, b1, b2 (implied by ".."), b3, b4, b5 (implied), b6, b7.
For .u4/.s4 types: 8 x 4-bit elements = 32 bits = one 32-bit register.
For .e2m1 type: 8 x 4-bit elements = 32 bits = one 32-bit register.
Elements b0 through b7 are packed sequentially within a single 32-bit register, with b0 in the least significant bits.

ARROW ANNOTATION:
A diagonal arrow in the figure spans from column 2 of row-group 0-7 down to column 2 of row-group 24-31, indicating that the pattern shown explicitly for columns 0, 1, and 7 continues uniformly across all 8 columns (0 through 7).

MMA .m16n8k64 Fragment Layout for Matrix B (rows 0-31), data types: .u4 / .s4 / .e2m1

OVERVIEW:
- Matrix: B
- Instruction: MMA .m16n8k64
- Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)
- This figure covers rows 0 through 31 of matrix B
- Grid structure: Rows represent groups of matrix rows, Columns (0, 1, 2, .., 7) represent register indices

GRID DIMENSIONS:
- Row groups: 4 groups (rows 0-7, rows 8-15, rows 16-23, rows 24-31)
- Columns shown: 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, indices 0 through 7)

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING:

Row group 0-7 (matrix rows 0 to 7):
  Column 0: Thread T0 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7 (8 elements packed in register)
  Column 1: Thread T4 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied continuation of pattern, arrow indicates column 2 maps to same pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T28 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row group 8-15 (matrix rows 8 to 15):
  Column 0: Thread T1 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T5 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T29 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row group 16-23 (matrix rows 16 to 23):
  Column 0: Thread T2 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T6 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T30 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row group 24-31 (matrix rows 24 to 31):
  Column 0: Thread T3 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T7 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, arrow from column 2 row 0-7 points down to column 2 row 24-31 indicating continuation)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T31 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN (column index to thread number):
  Column 0: T0 (rows 0-7), T1 (rows 8-15), T2 (rows 16-23), T3 (rows 24-31)
  Column 1: T4 (rows 0-7), T5 (rows 8-15), T6 (rows 16-23), T7 (rows 24-31)
  Column 2: (T8, T9, T10, T11 implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T28 (rows 0-7), T29 (rows 8-15), T30 (rows 16-23), T31 (rows 24-31)

General pattern: Thread number = (column_index * 4) + row_group_index
  where row_group_index = 0 for rows 0-7, 1 for rows 8-15, 2 for rows 16-23, 3 for rows 24-31

REGISTER PACKING:
Each thread holds 8 elements per register entry: b0, b1, b2 (implied by ".."), b3, b4, b5 (implied), b6, b7.
For .u4/.s4 types: 8 x 4-bit elements = 32 bits = one 32-bit register.
For .e2m1 type: 8 x 4-bit elements = 32 bits = one 32-bit register.
Elements b0 through b7 are packed sequentially within a single 32-bit register, with b0 in the least significant bits.

ARROW ANNOTATION:
A diagonal arrow in the figure spans from column 2 of row-group 0-7 down to column 2 of row-group 24-31, indicating that the pattern shown explicitly for columns 0, 1, and 7 continues uniformly across all 8 columns (0 through 7).


  Figure 94 MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for rows 0–31 of matrix B with `.u4` / `.s4` / `.e2m1` type.

  



[Figure: mma-16864-B_2]
MATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION

MMA Operation: .m16n8k64
Matrix: B (rows 32-63)
Data Types: .u4 / .s4 / .e2m1
This figure shows the SECOND half of matrix B (rows 32-63); the first half covers rows 0-31.

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (vertical axis): Matrix rows 32-63, divided into 4 bands of 8 rows each
- Columns (horizontal axis): Columns 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, indices 0 through 7)
- Threads shown: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)

TABLE OF THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING:

Row \ Col |  0   |  1   |  2  | .. |  7
----------|------|------|-----|----|------
32-39     | T0   | T4   | ... | .. | T28
40-47     | T1   | T5   | ... | .. | T29
48-55     | T2   | T6   | ... | .. | T30
56-63(64) | T3   | T7   | ... | .. | T31

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0, T1, T2, T3 cover columns 0 for row bands 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
- Threads T4, T5, T6, T7 cover column 1 for the same row bands respectively
- This pattern continues: threads T(4*col + row_band) cover (col, row_band)
- Threads T28, T29, T30, T31 cover column 7 for row bands 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively

REGISTER ELEMENTS PER THREAD:
Each thread holds a register group containing elements: b8, b9, .., b14, b15
Specifically, each thread's register contains 6 named elements (shown as a curly-brace vector):
  - b8
  - b9
  - .. (intermediate elements)
  - b14
  - b15

So each thread holds elements b8 through b15 (8 elements total: b8, b9, b10, b11, b12, b13, b14, b15).

ELEMENT PACKING:
- Data types are 4-bit (.u4/.s4) or 2-bit-mantissa (.e2m1)
- Multiple elements are packed into 32-bit registers
- For 4-bit types: 8 elements pack into one 32-bit register (b8 through b15 fit in one register)
- Elements b8-b15 represent the second set of 8 packed values (registers b0-b7 would be in rows 0-31)
- The register indices b8-b15 indicate these are the second register group (registers 8 through 15 of the B fragment)

DIAGONAL ARROW NOTE:
A diagonal arrow is drawn from the cell at approximately (col=2, rows=32-39) pointing down to (col=2, rows=56-63), indicating the continuation/ordering pattern of thread assignment across row bands within a column group.

COMPLETE THREAD ASSIGNMENT SUMMARY:
T0:  rows 32-39, col 0, elements b8-b15
T1:  rows 40-47, col 0, elements b8-b15
T2:  rows 48-55, col 0, elements b8-b15
T3:  rows 56-63, col 0, elements b8-b15
T4:  rows 32-39, col 1, elements b8-b15
T5:  rows 40-47, col 1, elements b8-b15
T6:  rows 48-55, col 1, elements b8-b15
T7:  rows 56-63, col 1, elements b8-b15
... (pattern continues for cols 2-6) ...
T28: rows 32-39, col 7, elements b8-b15
T29: rows 40-47, col 7, elements b8-b15
T30: rows 48-55, col 7, elements b8-b15
T31: rows 56-63, col 7, elements b8-b15

MATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION

MMA Operation: .m16n8k64
Matrix: B (rows 32-63)
Data Types: .u4 / .s4 / .e2m1
This figure shows the SECOND half of matrix B (rows 32-63); the first half covers rows 0-31.

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (vertical axis): Matrix rows 32-63, divided into 4 bands of 8 rows each
- Columns (horizontal axis): Columns 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, indices 0 through 7)
- Threads shown: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)

TABLE OF THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING:

Row \ Col |  0   |  1   |  2  | .. |  7
----------|------|------|-----|----|------
32-39     | T0   | T4   | ... | .. | T28
40-47     | T1   | T5   | ... | .. | T29
48-55     | T2   | T6   | ... | .. | T30
56-63(64) | T3   | T7   | ... | .. | T31

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0, T1, T2, T3 cover columns 0 for row bands 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
- Threads T4, T5, T6, T7 cover column 1 for the same row bands respectively
- This pattern continues: threads T(4*col + row_band) cover (col, row_band)
- Threads T28, T29, T30, T31 cover column 7 for row bands 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively

REGISTER ELEMENTS PER THREAD:
Each thread holds a register group containing elements: b8, b9, .., b14, b15
Specifically, each thread's register contains 6 named elements (shown as a curly-brace vector):
  - b8
  - b9
  - .. (intermediate elements)
  - b14
  - b15

So each thread holds elements b8 through b15 (8 elements total: b8, b9, b10, b11, b12, b13, b14, b15).

ELEMENT PACKING:
- Data types are 4-bit (.u4/.s4) or 2-bit-mantissa (.e2m1)
- Multiple elements are packed into 32-bit registers
- For 4-bit types: 8 elements pack into one 32-bit register (b8 through b15 fit in one register)
- Elements b8-b15 represent the second set of 8 packed values (registers b0-b7 would be in rows 0-31)
- The register indices b8-b15 indicate these are the second register group (registers 8 through 15 of the B fragment)

DIAGONAL ARROW NOTE:
A diagonal arrow is drawn from the cell at approximately (col=2, rows=32-39) pointing down to (col=2, rows=56-63), indicating the continuation/ordering pattern of thread assignment across row bands within a column group.

COMPLETE THREAD ASSIGNMENT SUMMARY:
T0:  rows 32-39, col 0, elements b8-b15
T1:  rows 40-47, col 0, elements b8-b15
T2:  rows 48-55, col 0, elements b8-b15
T3:  rows 56-63, col 0, elements b8-b15
T4:  rows 32-39, col 1, elements b8-b15
T5:  rows 40-47, col 1, elements b8-b15
T6:  rows 48-55, col 1, elements b8-b15
T7:  rows 56-63, col 1, elements b8-b15
... (pattern continues for cols 2-6) ...
T28: rows 32-39, col 7, elements b8-b15
T29: rows 40-47, col 7, elements b8-b15
T30: rows 48-55, col 7, elements b8-b15
T31: rows 56-63, col 7, elements b8-b15


  Figure 95 MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for rows 32–63 of matrix B with `.u4` / `.s4` / `.e2m1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7)          for bi where i < 8
             (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7) + 32     for bi where i >= 8

  col =   groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f32` | A vector expression containing four `.f32` registers, containing four `.f32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 96](#mma-16864-c).

  



[Figure: mma-16864-C]
MMA .m16n8k64 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32/.f32 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=64
Matrix role: Accumulator (C input / D output)
Data types: .s32 or .f32
Each thread holds 4 elements total, stored in 4 separate 32-bit registers (one element per register, since s32/f32 are not packed)
Elements per thread: c0, c1, c2, c3

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has rows labeled R (matrix rows 0-15) and columns labeled C (matrix columns 0-7).
Columns 0-1 form one group, columns 2-3 form a second group, columns 4-5 form a third group, columns 6-7 form a fourth group.

DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

Upper half of matrix (rows 0-7), elements c0 and c1:

Row 0, Cols 0-1: T0 holds {c0, c1}
Row 0, Cols 2-3: T1 holds {c0, c1}
Row 0, Cols 4-5: T2 holds {c0, c1}
Row 0, Cols 6-7: T3 holds {c0, c1}

Row 1, Cols 0-1: T4 holds {c0, c1}
Row 1, Cols 2-3: T5 holds {c0, c1}
Row 1, Cols 4-5: T6 holds {c0, c1}
Row 1, Cols 6-7: T7 holds {c0, c1}

Rows 2-6: Pattern continues with threads T8 through T27 (implied by arrows showing continuation), each group of 4 threads covering one row, 2 elements per thread pair of columns.

Row 7, Cols 0-1: T28 holds {c0, c1}
Row 7, Cols 2-3: T29 holds {c0, c1}
Row 7, Cols 4-5: T30 holds {c0, c1}
Row 7, Cols 6-7: T31 holds {c0, c1}

Lower half of matrix (rows 8-15), elements c2 and c3:

Row 8, Cols 0-1: T0 holds {c2, c3}
Row 8, Cols 2-3: T1 holds {c2, c3}
Row 8, Cols 4-5: T2 holds {c2, c3}
Row 8, Cols 6-7: T3 holds {c2, c3}

Row 9, Cols 0-1: T4 holds {c2, c3}
Row 9, Cols 2-3: T5 holds {c2, c3}
Row 9, Cols 4-5: T6 holds {c2, c3}
Row 9, Cols 6-7: T7 holds {c2, c3}

Rows 10-14: Pattern continues with threads T8 through T27 (implied by arrows).

Row 15, Cols 0-1: T28 holds {c2, c3}
Row 15, Cols 2-3: T29 holds {c2, c3}
Row 15, Cols 4-5: T30 holds {c2, c3}
Row 15, Cols 6-7: T31 holds {c2, c3}

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
Threads T0-T31 each cover two column positions across both halves of the matrix.
Each thread covers exactly 2 columns in the N=8 dimension.
Rows 0-7 map to register elements c0, c1.
Rows 8-15 map to register elements c2, c3.
Each row of the matrix is covered by 4 threads (one thread per 2-column group).
The same 32 threads (T0-T31) repeat for both the upper 8 rows and lower 8 rows, distinguished only by which register elements (c0,c1 vs c2,c3) are used.

REGISTER PACKING
For .s32 and .f32 types, each element occupies one full 32-bit register.
c0, c1, c2, c3 are each stored in separate registers (no packing of two values into one register).
Total registers per thread for accumulator: 4 registers (one each for c0, c1, c2, c3).

  Figure 96 MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for accumulator matrix C/D with `.s32` / `.f32` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                           for ci where i <  2
             groupID + 8                       for ci where i >= 2

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci  where i = {0,..,3}
  ```
